title: Intensely Resinous Russian Pasta
description: A crisp and resinous monsoon Russian dish featuring paccheri pasta, long-fermentation-dough, and a dash-friendly flavor profile, all wrapped in a corn husk vessel.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large mixing bowl, combine the long-fermentation-dough, olive oil, resinous herb mix, garlic, salt, and black pepper, and mix until a sticky dough forms.
Knead the dough for 10 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Wrap the dough in plastic wrap and let it rest for 30 minutes.
Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil and cook the paccheri pasta according to the package instructions until al dente.
Drain the pasta and set it aside.
Soak the corn husks in water for 30 minutes to make them pliable.
Assemble the dish by placing a portion of pasta in the center of a corn husk, topping it with a spoonful of the dough mixture, and sprinkling with parmesan cheese.
Fold the corn husk over the filling to form a tight packet and place it on a baking sheet.
Repeat the process with the remaining ingredients and bake the packets in the preheated oven for 20 minutes.
